title: The main risk caching introduces
prompt_md: You add a cache in front of your database and it makes the app much faster. In an interview you're asked: **"What is the main problem that caching introduces?"** Which answer is correct?
options: Stale data — the cache can serve an old value after the underlying source has changed, and knowing exactly when/what to invalidate is genuinely hard
Caching always makes the application slower because of the extra network hop
Caching uses more database connections than not caching at all
Caching is illegal to use in production systems without a license
explanation_md: The instant you cache a value you create a second copy of the truth. When the source changes, the cached copy is **stale** until it expires or is invalidated. Cache **invalidation** — knowing every key that depends on the changed data and evicting all of them — is famously one of the hardest problems in computer science. The speed is real, but correctness/freshness is the price you manage with short TTLs and explicit invalidation on writes.
